stories.title: D49 Expands Early Childhood Education — 2 New ECE Classrooms Opening August 2026
stories.angle: District 49 is opening two new Early Childhood Education classrooms at the D49 Student Success Center in August 2026, expanding access to pre-K programs in the Falcon/Peyton/Calhan area. Separately, 9 D49 campuses (7 operated, 2 charter) have earned Purple Star School designation from the Colorado Department of Education — recognition for exemplary support of military-connected students and families. COS has a significant military family population (Ft. Carson, Schriever, Peterson).

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
D49 families: two new Early Childhood Education classrooms are opening at the Student Success Center in August 2026. Plus, 9 D49 campuses just earned Purple Star recognition for supporting military-connected students.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
If you're a military family in D49, Purple Star Schools are designed with you in mind. Nine D49 campuses now carry that designation. More details at d49.org.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Big news for D49 families this spring: District 49 is opening two new Early Childhood Education classrooms at the D49 Student Success Center in August 2026 — just in time for the new school year.

The new classrooms expand D49's pre-K access at a time when more families are looking for quality early childhood programming before kindergarten. The Student Success Center itself opens at the start of the 2026-27 school year, and these two ECE classrooms will be part of that launch.

There's more good news: nine D49 campuses (seven district-operated schools plus two charter schools) have earned Purple Star School designation from the Colorado Department of Education. The Purple Star designation recognizes schools that go above and beyond to support military-connected students and families — a meaningful recognition in a city with Fort Carson, Schriever Space Force Base, and Peterson Space Force Base in its backyard.

If you're a military family in D49, this designation is worth knowing. It means your child's school has staff trained to support transitions, has resources for mobile families, and has committed to making military kids feel welcomed and supported.

For enrollment or more details on the new ECE classrooms, visit d49.org or contact your school's front office. What questions do you have about D49's early childhood options?
facebook.image_caption: Two new Early Childhood Education classrooms are opening at the D49 Student Success Center in August 2026. Nine D49 campuses have also earned Purple Star School recognition for supporting military-connected families. Big wins for D49 families this spring.

article.headline: D49 Opens 2 New Early Childhood Education Classrooms This Fall — Plus, 9 Campuses Earn Purple Star Recognition

stories.title: CPSC Recall — Bellabu Bear Children's Sherpa Robes (Burn Hazard)
stories.angle: The CPSC has announced a recall of Bellabu Bear bamboo sherpa children's robes due to a burn hazard — the robes violate mandatory flammability standards for children's sleepwear. The robes were sold at Saks Fifth Avenue and online at Bellabubear.com and Saksfifthavenue.com from January 2024 through July 2025 for about $60. Children's sizes S/M and M/L. Prints include PAW Patrol, Minecraft, Minions, Batman, and others. Consumers should immediately stop use and contact Bellabu Bear for a full refund.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
CPSC recall: Bellabu Bear sherpa kids' robes fail flammability standards — burn hazard. Prints: PAW Patrol, Minecraft, Minions + more. Sold at Saks + online Jan 2024–July 2025, ~$60. Full refund. 888-703-7752.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Heads up for parents who bought Bellabu Bear bamboo sherpa robes for their kids: there's a CPSC recall you need to know about.

The Consumer Product Safety Commission has announced that Bellabu Bear's bamboo sherpa children's robes violate mandatory flammability standards for children's sleepwear, creating a burn hazard. No incidents or injuries have been reported — but the recall is happening now so we can act before anything goes wrong.

Here's what to look for: the robes come in children's sizes S/M and M/L, have sherpa lining, two front pockets, and a matching belt. The neck label reads "Bellabu Bear," "BB-NW12109," and the size. The prints include popular characters like PAW Patrol, Minecraft, Minions, Batman, Justice League, Wonder Woman, Blaze and the Monster Machines, Trolls, Dora, and Nutcracker, along with holiday and novelty designs (hearts, Santa, stripes, bows, dragons).

These robes were sold at Saks Fifth Avenue stores and online at Bellabubear.com, Letthembelittleboutique.com, and Saksfifthavenue.com between January 2024 and July 2025 for about $60.

If you have one: stop using it immediately, remove it from your child's access, and contact Bellabu Bear for a full refund or store credit plus a prepaid return shipping label. You can reach them at 888-703-7752 (9 AM–4 PM PT, Monday–Friday) or at help@bellabubear.com.

Safety first — please share this with anyone who might have one of these.
facebook.image_caption: CPSC recall: Bellabu Bear bamboo sherpa children's robes — burn hazard due to flammability standards violation. Popular character prints (PAW Patrol, Minecraft, Minions + more). Sold ~$60 at Saks + online, Jan 2024–July 2025. Full refund available. Contact: 888-703-7752 or help@bellabubear.com.

article.headline: CPSC Recall: Bellabu Bear Children's Sherpa Robes Fail Flammability Standards — Full Refund Available

stories.title: Spring Activity Guide for COS Families — Events This Week and Next
stories.angle: April is a prime time to get families outside in Colorado Springs, with temperatures warming and nature programming ramping up. Coming events: Early Literacy Event for families with deaf/hard-of-hearing children (April 17, 1:30-3 PM, Bear Creek Nature Center, birth-8 years); Bird Banding at Bear Creek (April 18, ages 8+); Fountain Creek Regional Park habitat exploration (April 24, 2-4 PM, all ages). Average high ~59°F, 9-11 hours of sunshine daily this week.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
COS families — mark your calendars:

Apr 17 — Bear Creek Early Literacy (deaf/hard-of-hearing kids, birth–8, 1:30 PM)
Apr 18 — Bird Banding at Bear Creek (ages 8+)
Apr 24 — Fountain Creek habitat exploration (2–4 PM)

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
COS weather is cooperating this week — 🌤 highs near 59°F and 9+ hours of sunshine daily. If you've been waiting for the right moment to get your family outside, this is it.

Here's a roundup of nature programs and events coming up at COS parks this week and next:

🌿 **April 17, 1:30–3:00 PM — Bear Creek Nature Center**
Early Literacy Event for families with deaf and hard-of-hearing children, birth through 8 years old. Includes reading in American Sign Language, wildlife learning, and a nature walk. This one's a gem — a rare inclusive programming event in a beautiful outdoor setting.

🐦 **April 18 — Bear Creek Nature Center**
Bird Banding — no birding experience required, recommended for ages 8 and up. A hands-on way to learn about bird migration and conservation with local naturalists.

💧 **April 24, 2:00–4:00 PM — Fountain Creek Regional Park**
Habitat exploration event where families discover how different habitats are shaped by water. Great for curious kids who want to know why the world looks the way it does.

All three of these are run by Colorado Springs Parks programs. If you're not already following the city's parks and rec calendar, it's worth a bookmark — they run good stuff. What's your family's favorite COS outdoor spot this time of year?
facebook.image_caption: Spring is here and COS parks are busy. Bear Creek Early Literacy Event (April 17), Bird Banding (April 18), Fountain Creek habitat exploration (April 24). Get outside this week.

stories.title: AAP Screen Time Shift — Quality Over Quantity (What COS Families Need to Know)
stories.angle: The American Academy of Pediatrics updated its screen time guidance in January 2026, moving away from strict hour limits toward a framework based on content quality, interactivity, and whether screens are replacing essential activities (sleep, movement, family connection). The AAP Advocacy Conference just concluded April 12-14 in Arlington, VA. Three key questions every parent should ask: Is content age-appropriate and high quality? Is it interactive or passive? Is it replacing something more valuable?

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
AAP updated screen time guidance in 2026. No strict hour limits. Ask 3 questions: Is it quality content? Interactive? Replacing sleep or play?

No screens under 18 months (except video chat).

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
The two-hours-per-day rule has been a fixture of parenting advice for the better part of a decade. In January 2026, the American Academy of Pediatrics officially updated their guidance — and the shift is worth understanding.

The new framework isn't about counting minutes. It's about asking three questions when your kids are on screens:

**1. Is the content high quality and age-appropriate?**
Higher-quality experiences model social-emotional skills, have clear learning goals, and allow space for creativity. Lower-quality passive content (endless autoplay, etc.) is the concern.

**2. Is it interactive or passive?**
Interactive content — where kids respond, problem-solve, or extend learning offline — is associated with better outcomes than passive watching. Co-viewing and talking about what they're watching matters.

**3. Is it replacing something more valuable?**
Screens that cut into sleep, physical activity, family mealtime, or free play are the real red flags. Device-free bedrooms and device-free mealtimes still make sense.

The AAP still recommends no screens before 18 months (video chat is the exception). For ages 2–5, they moved away from the previous 1-hour-per-day limit and toward this quality-focused framework.

What this means practically: a 40-minute episode of a well-made nature show watched together and talked about afterward is different from 40 minutes of algorithmically-served shorts. The clock matters less than the context.

Have you changed how you think about screen time in your house? We're curious what's working for COS families.
facebook.image_caption: The AAP updated screen time guidance in 2026. No more strict hour limits — it's now about content quality, whether it's interactive, and whether it's replacing better activities. The full framework (and what it means for your family) is in our latest article.

article.headline: The AAP Changed Screen Time Guidance in 2026 — Here's What It Actually Means for Your Family

research.facts:
AAP Screen Time Guidelines Update 2026
**Sources:**
- aap.org/en/patient-care/media-and-children/ (PRIMARY — official AAP)
- edsurge.com/news/2026-02-05-new-aap-screen-time-recommendations-focus-less-on-screens-more-on-family-time (PRIMARY — February 2026 news article)
- health.choc.org/updated-aap-recommendations-for-screen-time/ (SECONDARY — CHOC health summary)
- kidsai.app/blog/new-screen-time-guidelines-2026-what-parents-need-to-know (SECONDARY)
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence — 2+ sources):**
- AAP updated screen time guidance in January/early 2026 [EdSurge Feb 2026 + CHOC + kidsai.app]
- Shift from strict hour limits to quality, context, and conversation framework [AAP official + EdSurge + CHOC]
- Previous guideline (2016): limit to 2 hours/day for ages 2+ [multiple sources]
- New framework asks 3 questions: (1) Is content high quality and age-appropriate? (2) Is it interactive or passive? (3) Is it replacing sleep, movement, or real-world connection? [AAP + EdSurge + CHOC]
- No set screen time limit for ages 2–5 under new guidance (replaces 1-hour-daily recommendation) [AAP + CHOC]
- Still: no screens before 18 months except video chat [AAP + CHOC]
- Family media plans still recommended over arbitrary limits [AAP official]
- Device-free bedrooms and mealtimes recommended [AAP + CHOC]
- AAP Advocacy Conference: April 12-14, 2026, Arlington, VA [aap.org conference listing]
**Verified Facts (MEDIUM confidence — single source):**
- "Rules focusing on balance, content, co-viewing, and communication are associated with better well-being outcomes than rules focused on screen time" [AAP policy per CHOC summary]

stories.title: D11 Kindergarten Roundup Season — What Families with Rising Kindergarteners Need to Do Now
stories.angle: Spring is kindergarten roundup season in Colorado Springs, and D11 families with 5-year-olds entering fall 2026 need to act now. D11 typically holds kindergarten roundup events and enrollment windows in spring for the following school year. With D11 managing school transitions including the Orton Academy co-location at Trailblazer, families in the Westside/Central areas should confirm their neighborhood school assignment and enrollment status. Key items: proof of address, immunization records, birth certificate, and completed enrollment form.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
D11 families: kindergarten roundup season is here. Your child must turn 5 by Oct 1, 2026 to enroll for fall. You'll need a birth certificate, immunization records, and proof of address. Find your school at d11.org.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Spring means kindergarten roundup season in Colorado Springs, and if you have a child who will turn 5 by October 1, 2026, now is the time to get organized.

Here's your checklist for D11 kindergarten enrollment:

✅ **Confirm your child is eligible.** In Colorado, children must turn 5 on or before October 1, 2026 to enroll in fall 2026 kindergarten.

✅ **Gather your documents.** You'll need: your child's birth certificate, up-to-date immunization records (or a Colorado Certificate of Immunization Exemption), and proof of your current address (utility bill, lease, or similar).

✅ **Find your neighborhood school.** Use the school boundary tool at d11.org to confirm which D11 school serves your address. Families in the Westside and west-central areas should double-check school assignments given the upcoming changes at Trailblazer Elementary (Orton Academy co-location starting fall 2026).

✅ **Complete enrollment.** D11 uses an online enrollment system — check d11.org for the enrollment window and portal link. Some schools also hold in-person kindergarten roundup events where kids can visit their new classroom before the year starts.

✅ **Ask questions early.** If you have questions about specialty programs (dual language, gifted/talented, Montessori), call the school directly or contact D11's enrollment team before the window closes.

The earlier you start, the smoother the transition. If you know a family who's new to D11 and navigating this for the first time, share this along. What questions do you have about D11 kindergarten enrollment?
facebook.image_caption: D11 families: kindergarten roundup season is here. Your child must turn 5 by October 1, 2026 to enroll for fall. Birth certificate, immunization records, and proof of address are your three must-haves. Check d11.org for your school and enrollment dates.

article.headline: D11 Kindergarten Enrollment Checklist for Fall 2026 — Everything You Need to Do This Spring
